content.problemStatement:
Alice has a standard deck of 525252 cards. She wants to play a card game with K−1K-1K−1 of her friends. This particular game requires each person to have an equal number of cards, so Alice needs to discard a certain number of cards from her deck so that she can equally distribute the remaining cards amongst her and her friends.

Find the **minimum** number of cards Alice has to discard such that she can play the game.

**Note:** It doesn't matter which person gets which card. The only thing that matters is that each person should have an equal number of cards.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
Each test case consists of a single line of input, containing a single integer KKK — the number of people playing the game (including Alice).


Output Format

For each test case, output in a single line the **minimum** number of cards Alice has to discard from her deck such that she can play the game with her friends.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer K (2 ≤ K ≤ 52) – the total number of people (Alice plus her friends).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the minimum number of cards Alice must discard so that the remaining cards can be divided equally among K people.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For K=3, 52%3=1 discard 1 card. For K=50, 52%50=2 discard 2 cards. For K=2, 52%2=0 discard none. For K=14, 52%14=10 discard 10 cards.
52 is already divisible by 52, so no discard is needed.
52%7=3, discard 3 cards to have 49 cards, which can be given 7 each.

solution.approach: Alice has a fixed deck of 52 cards. To distribute the cards equally among K people we need the remaining number of cards to be a multiple of K. The smallest number of cards to discard is the remainder when 52 is divided by K, i.e., 52 % K. If the remainder is zero, no discard is needed. For each test case we simply compute this remainder.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – each test case requires O(1) arithmetic.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.

code.C:
#include <stdio.h>
int main(){int T; if(scanf("%d",&T)!=1) return 0; while(T--){int K; scanf("%d",&K); printf("%d\n", 52 % K);} return 0;}
